channel.title: Quinta & Co. Blog
channel.description: Practical notes on running a business and using AI, for women founders.

item.title: What Does It Actually Cost to Form an LLC in Texas?
item.description: The only required cost is the $300 filing fee. Everything else — the $99 EIN, the $249 agent, the $2,000 package — is optional, and most of it is markup. Here's the real math.

item.title: You Don't Need to Understand AI to Use It. You Need to Know Where to Start.
item.description: "I'm not technical." "Too expensive." "No time." None of the three is really about AI — they're about starting. Here are the three low-stakes tasks to start with.

item.title: More Women Are Starting Businesses Than Ever — Here's What AI Actually Changed.
item.description: More women are starting businesses than ever, because AI dropped the cost of trying — but starting isn't the same as staying open. Here's what actually changed, and what didn't.

item.title: What 3 Women Building AI Say Comes Next (And What It Means for Yours)
item.description: Mira Murati, Fei-Fei Li, and the women shaping AI's next chapter aren't talking about robots replacing you.

item.title: Three AI Quick Wins Every Woman Business Owner Should Start With
item.description: Skip the strategy sessions and automation overwhelm. Start with the pain that keeps you up at 2 AM — and the simple tools that solve it in under four hours.

item.title: The AI gap that's costing women-owned businesses everything
item.description: Women adopt AI at half the rate of men, but our businesses are growing 3x faster. The math doesn't add up — and neither does ignoring it.

item.title: Why I'm Not Replacing Experience
item.description: After leaving corporate advertising to co-found a construction company, I found AI's real value isn't replacing expertise — it's recreating the departmental support I lost.
